perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that each owns CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed list of items considered as Confidential Information, it creates ambiguity and contradicts the definition in section 1 of Non-Disclosure Agreement where it is mentioned that CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION includes discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary. No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction. Specific aspects or details of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed list of what is considered Confidential Information. The changed text removes this specific list, creating ambiguity about what constitutes Confidential Information. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary'. By omitting the detailed list, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it fails to properly identify/define what Confidential Information entails, making the application of the definition in the contradicted text uncertain and potentially unenforceable. This is an example of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' and 'being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION', it weakens the agreement and creates a contradiction. While they are obligated to treat as confidential, there is no way to 'preserve the confidentiality', which undermines the intended protection. Also, the officers and employees are not obligated by a written obligation to maintain confidentiality, which also makes it hard to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA. Any Party may also cause such disclosures to be made to the other Party on behalf of the disclosing Party by third parties who are Under obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; such disclosures from third parties shall be deemed to be disclosures by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit obligations to 'preserve the confidentiality' and to ensure officers and employees are 'bound by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows a party to disclose confidential information to another party, or cause such disclosures to be made by third parties who are 'Under obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party'. The omission in the Changed Text now creates a situation where the receiving party, or the third party, may not be under sufficient obligation to maintain confidentiality, thus directly contradicting the assumption in the contradicted text that such obligations exist.

perturbation.original_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause requiring the return of confidential information upon request, the contract now contradicts the general expectation of confidentiality and the right of the disclosing party to retrieve their information. The absence of a return clause creates uncertainty about what happens to the information after the JRA's term and goes against typical NDA practice. This creates a contradiction with paragraph 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the receiving party to return confidential information upon request, ensuring the disclosing party's control and confidentiality. The changed text removes this obligation, directly contradicting the contradicted text's emphasis on treating information as confidential, limiting its use, and restricting disclosures. The removal creates a scenario where the receiving party is no longer explicitly obligated to return the confidential information, undermining the confidentiality provisions outlined in the contradicted section. This absence conflicts with the intended protection and creates a contradiction regarding the handling of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will continue indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the expiration of obligations from 'five years from the termination of the JRA' to 'continue indefinitely,' it fundamentally alters the agreement. The original contract contemplated an end to the confidentiality obligations, but now those obligations persist forever. This creates an obvious contradiction with the original intent and adds ambiguity to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and provisions of this Agreement will inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will be binding on said successors and assigns. This paragraph notwithstanding, neither Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign absent prior written consent of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the obligations expire five years from termination of the JRA. The changed text states the obligations continue indefinitely. The contradicted text refers to the agreement benefiting and binding successors and assigns, but restricts disclosing confidential information without consent. By changing the expiration to 'indefinitely,' a contradiction arises. The contradicted section contemplates potential successors and assigns, implying a possible end at some point, aligning with the original 5-year expiration. The changed text removes any such end date, creating an irreconcilable contradiction regarding the duration and applicability of the obligations to successors and assigns because the agreement now lasts indefinitely.
